_comment: EAS credentials structure reference. Do NOT populate this file with real values. Use `eas credentials` CLI or the EAS dashboard to upload credentials to EAS Vault. See docs/runbook.md §17.

android._comment: Android keystore. Upload via: eas credentials --platform android. EAS stores it in Vault — you do NOT need a local keystore file for cloud builds (credentialsSource=remote).
_notes: Generate a new keystore per tenant: keytool -genkey -v -keystore <tenant>.keystore -alias <alias> -keyalg RSA -keysize 2048 -validity 10000
Store the keystore file in a secure vault (e.g., Azure Key Vault, 1Password) — NOT in git.
The Play Store ties the app identity to this keystore forever. Losing it means losing the ability to update the app.
For Play App Signing (recommended): upload the upload keystore to EAS, then let Google manage the distribution key.

ios._comment: iOS distribution requires an Apple Developer Program membership per tenant (or a shared platform account). Deferred until first iOS build is required.
_notes: iOS credentials are managed via EAS Vault with credentialsSource=remote.
Run `eas credentials --platform ios` to have EAS generate or import certificates and profiles.
App Store Connect API key is required for automated submission — create at https://appstoreconnect.apple.com/access/api
